proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_057 (ordinal, levels 0/1/2) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response in this AML cohort, consistent with feature_057 representing a disease-severity or prognostic-risk classifier.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
x=df['feature_057']
X=sm.add_constant(x)
m=sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 ORR by level: 0=0.213 (n=17592), 1=0.155 (n=24971), 2=0.112 (n=7437). Logistic regression coefficient per level = -0.260 (OR per unit = 0.771); p=1.84e-97. Strong dose-response decrease in objective_response with higher feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_011 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 in responders=3.383 vs non-responders=3.952. Logistic regression coefficient per SD = -0.155; p=7.43e-35. Strong negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_006 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_006 in responders=58.23 vs non-responders=60.31. Logistic coefficient per SD = -0.109; p=1.16e-19. Higher feature_006 lowers ORR.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035 = 1 have a higher rate of objective_response than those with feature_035 = 0, consistent with feature_035 marking a favorable biomarker or active treatment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_035=1: ORR=0.2213 (n=3624); feature_035=0: ORR=0.1650 (n=46376); risk diff = +0.0563. Logistic OR=1.439; p=4.13e-18. Strong positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_099 are associated with a higher probability of objective_response (positive direction).

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_099 in responders=3.825 vs non-responders=3.796. Logistic coefficient per SD = +0.058; p=1.36e-06. Modest positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_063 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_063 in responders=5.654 vs non-responders=6.166. Logistic coefficient per SD = -0.064; p=2.82e-06.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_092 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic coefficient per SD of feature_092 = -0.056; p=1.88e-05. Higher feature_092 lowers ORR.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_093 = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than those with feature_093 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_093=1: ORR=0.1848 (n=4025); feature_093=0: ORR=0.1677 (n=45975); risk diff = +0.0172. Logistic coef=0.118; p=0.00529. Modest positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_121 = 1 have a lower probability of objective_response than those with feature_121 = 0.
Patients with feature_014 = 1 have a slightly higher probability of objective_response than those with feature_014 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_014=1: ORR=0.1735 (n=20020); feature_014=0: ORR=0.1661 (n=29980); risk diff = +0.0074. Logistic coef=0.053; p=0.0306. Small positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rates differ by patient race (feature_005), with the lowest rates in patients categorized as black or other and the highest rates in white patients.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_005: white=0.1728 (n=32498), hispanic=0.1670 (n=7631), asian=0.1633 (n=2970), black=0.1574 (n=5889), other=0.1472 (n=1012). Chi-square chi2=13.37, p=0.009595. Significant overall difference; ordering: white > hispanic > asian > black > other.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rates differ by insurance type (feature_087), with patients on medicaid or who are uninsured having lower ORR than those with private or medicare insurance.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_087: medicare=0.1718, private=0.1666, medicaid=0.1687, uninsured=0.1662. Chi-square p=0.5403. NOT significant — insurance type alone shows no meaningful unadjusted association with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078) is associated with objective_response.
feature_044 (continuous, range 6-18) is associated with objective_response.
feature_018 (ordinal 0-10) shows a monotonic association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_078 mean in responders=68.03 vs non-responders=67.98. Logistic coef per SD = 0.0043; p=0.718. Not significant — age has no detectable main effect on objective_response.
feature_044 mean in responders=12.48 vs non-responders=12.51. Logistic coef per SD = -0.0172; p=0.149. Not significant.
feature_018 ordinal coef=0.0114; p=0.34. Not significant; no monotonic association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: High-prevalence binary features feature_007, feature_017, feature_002, feature_122, feature_025, feature_070, and feature_085 each show a main-effect difference in objective_response between their 0 and 1 strata.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic for each feature: feature_007 ORR1=0.1709 vs ORR0=0.1675, p=0.32, feature_017 ORR1=0.1680 vs ORR0=0.1699, p=0.564, feature_002 ORR1=0.1674 vs ORR0=0.1705, p=0.363, feature_122 ORR1=0.1683 vs ORR0=0.1696, p=0.693, feature_025 ORR1=0.1680 vs ORR0=0.1699, p=0.588, feature_070 ORR1=0.1702 vs ORR0=0.1681, p=0.532, feature_085 ORR1=0.1680 vs ORR0=0.1703, p=0.497. None of these high-prevalence binaries show a significant main effect — they appear to be unrelated covariates rather than treatment- or response-defining markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the other strong univariate predictors (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099, feature_057, feature_093, race, and insurance), feature_035 = 1 retains an independent positive effect on objective_response.
After adjustment for clinical covariates, feature_057 retains an independent ordinal negative effect on objective_response.

analyses.code:
X=pd.concat([cont_z, df[bin_top].astype(int), df['feature_057'], race_dummies, ins_dummies], axis=1)
m=sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression: feature_035 adjusted OR=1.436 (p=1.34e-17); feature_057 adjusted OR per level=0.681 (p=8.04e-97); feature_011 OR/SD=0.856 (p=1.47e-34); feature_006 OR/SD=0.896 (p=9.23e-20); feature_099 OR/SD=1.061 (p=9.67e-07); feature_063 OR/SD=0.935 (p=1.19e-06); feature_092 OR/SD=0.944 (p=1.03e-05); feature_093 OR=1.135 (p=0.00315). All retain significance after mutual adjustment; effects are independent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_035, feature_093), non-white patients (feature_005 != white) still have lower probability of objective_response than white patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted nonwhite vs white: coef=-0.0764, OR=0.926, p=0.002654. Statistically significant residual disparity: ~7.4% lower odds of response for non-white patients after adjusting for measured clinical covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response varies across feature_057 severity levels — specifically, the absolute risk difference attributable to feature_035 is larger in patients with low feature_057 (less severe) than in those with high feature_057 (most severe).

analyses.code: Xf=pd.concat([f35, f57, f35*f57], axis=1); m=sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(Xf)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: ORR by stratum: f57=0/f35=0=0.2082, f57=0/f35=1=0.2697 (RD=+0.0615); f57=1/f35=0=0.1514, f57=1/f35=1=0.2048 (RD=+0.0535); f57=2/f35=0=0.1088, f57=2/f35=1=0.1531 (RD=+0.0443). Logistic interaction term feature_035 × feature_057 coef=+0.0270, p=0.674. On the log-odds scale, no significant interaction; the OR for feature_035 is similar at every severity level. On the absolute-risk scale the benefit appears larger at low severity (~6 pp) vs high severity (~4 pp), but this attenuation is consistent with a non-interactive log-odds model and is not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 = 1 on objective_response is greater in white patients than in non-white patients (a feature_005 × feature_035 interaction).

analyses.code: X_full=concat(f035, race_d, race_d*f035); X_red=concat(f035, race_d); LR test
analyses.result_summary: Risk difference by race: white=+0.0646, asian=+0.0396, black=+0.0409, hispanic=+0.0410, other=+0.0542. Likelihood-ratio test of race × feature_035 interaction: chi2=2.056, df=4, p=0.726. No significant interaction — feature_035's effect is approximately constant across racial groups; numerical differences are within sampling noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association of feature_011 with objective_response is stronger (steeper slope) at higher feature_057 severity levels.

analyses.code: Xf=pd.concat([f11z, f57, f11z*f57], axis=1); sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(Xf)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logistic interaction feature_011 (per SD) × feature_057 coef = -0.0072; p=0.71. NOT significant — feature_011's effect is uniform across severity strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association of feature_011 with objective_response is stronger in patients with feature_121 = 1 than in those with feature_121 = 0 (i.e. there is a feature_011 × feature_121 interaction).

analyses.code: Xf=pd.concat([f11z, f121, f11z*f121], axis=1); sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(Xf)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logistic interaction feature_011 (per SD) × feature_121 coef = -0.1300; p=0.00773. Statistically significant: feature_011's negative effect on objective_response is amplified in feature_121=1 patients. Direction: more negative slope when feature_121=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of feature_099 with objective_response is attenuated (or reversed) in patients with feature_035 = 1.

analyses.code: Xf=pd.concat([f99z, f35, f99z*f35], axis=1); sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(Xf)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logistic interaction feature_099 (per SD) × feature_035 coef = -0.0936; p=0.0239. Borderline significant negative interaction — feature_099's positive main effect is partly cancelled out among patients with feature_035=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative associations of feature_006 and feature_092 with objective_response are super-additive (multiplicative) — patients with high values of both face a worse-than-additive penalty (negative continuous–continuous interaction on log-odds).

analyses.code: Xf=pd.concat([f6z, f92z, f6z*f92z], axis=1); sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(Xf)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logistic interaction feature_006 (per SD) × feature_092 (per SD) coef = -0.0358; p=0.00564. Statistically significant negative interaction: when both feature_006 and feature_092 are simultaneously elevated, the joint penalty on objective_response is greater than either main effect alone would predict.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The race effect on objective_response (feature_005) varies by insurance type (feature_087); equivalently, the insurance effect varies by race.

analyses.code: race_d * ins_d cross terms; LR test full vs reduced
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test feature_005 × feature_087 interaction: chi2=22.92, df=12, p=0.02844. Statistically significant overall — the race effect is heterogeneous across insurance types, although individual cell estimates are noisy at this granularity (subgroups e.g. uninsured/other have small n).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_057 (severity), white patients have higher objective_response rates than black patients.

analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0 (low severity): white ORR=0.2174 (n=11401) vs black ORR=0.2046 (n=2063); RD=+0.0129; nonwhite logit p=0.0452. feature_057=1: white=0.1581 (n=16270) vs black=0.1428 (n=2956); RD=+0.0154; nonwhite p=0.077. feature_057=2: white=0.1170 (n=4827) vs black=0.0954 (n=870); RD=+0.0216; nonwhite p=0.0543. Within each severity stratum, white patients consistently exhibit higher response rates than black/non-white patients, supporting that the race effect persists across severity strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest, mutually-independent predictors of objective_response in this AML cohort are: (a) higher feature_057 severity (negative); (b) higher feature_011 (negative); (c) higher feature_006 (negative); (d) higher feature_063 (negative); (e) higher feature_092 (negative); (f) higher feature_099 (positive); (g) feature_035 = 1 (positive, treatment/biomarker-like); (h) feature_093 = 1 (positive); and a residual non-white vs white disparity persists after adjustment for these clinical features.

analyses.code: Final multivariable logistic regression with all top predictors plus race/insurance, plus stratified race analysis
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted ORs (all p<0.05 except race): feature_057 OR/level=0.681 (p=8.04e-97); feature_011 OR/SD=0.856 (p=1.47e-34); feature_006 OR/SD=0.896 (p=9.23e-20); feature_099 OR/SD=1.061 (p=9.67e-07); feature_063 OR/SD=0.935 (p=1.19e-06); feature_092 OR/SD=0.944 (p=1.03e-05); feature_035 OR=1.436 (p=1.34e-17); feature_093 OR=1.135 (p=0.00315); adjusted nonwhite vs white OR=0.926 (p=0.00265). Conclusion: feature_057 (likely a severity/risk classifier) and continuous markers feature_011 and feature_006 dominate the prognostic signal; feature_035 (binary) confers a robust ~44% increased odds of response and behaves like a favorable treatment or molecular marker; non-white race is associated with a modest (~7%) residual reduction in odds of response after adjustment for measured clinical covariates.
